highlights_headline: SkiaSharp 2.88.1 adds WinUI, Skottie, AVIF and tvOS/Tizen TFMs on top of the 2.88 native stack.
highlights_body: The first servicing release for 2.88 refreshes bundled native libraries (libjpeg-turbo, libwebp, libexpat, zlib), splits Skottie and SceneGraph into their own packages, and rounds out the .NET 6 target matrix with WinUI, Tizen, iOS/Android/macOS TFMs for Skottie, and nullable attributes across the API.

bullets.detail: Skottie and SceneGraph move out of the core `SkiaSharp.dll` into their own packages, gain `net6.0` TFMs for iOS/Android/Catalyst/macOS/tvOS/Tizen, and pick up `TimeSpan` overloads, nullable attributes and non-seekable stream support.
The `SKEncodedImageFormat` surface now exposes AVIF so images can be encoded to AVIF where the underlying decoder supports it.
SkiaSharp views now light up on WinUI, and the Uno WinUI target ships the missing JavaScript support file.

bullets.lead: Writable SKDataStream and animations with a BOM
bullets.detail: `SKDataStream` is now writable like `SKData`, and animation loaders tolerate a leading byte-order mark.

bullets.lead: Nullable attributes and new TFMs on the binding
bullets.detail: The binding project adds nullable annotations plus newer TFMs, and the `net6.0-tizen` TFM is added.
The bundled zlib was updated to pick up upstream decode fixes that resolved PNG loading failures.

bullets.lead: Fonts with Unicode filenames load correctly
bullets.detail: Font loaders no longer fail when the font's file name contains Unicode characters.
`SKGLView` on macOS now reshapes when the window's backing scale factor changes so content stays crisp on scale transitions.
Internal critical sections now check whether the lock has been finalized before entering, avoiding rare shutdown exceptions.
`SKShaper` now respects the `TextAlign` on the supplied `SKPaint` when laying out shaped text.

bullets.lead: Resize returns null on invalid bounds
bullets.detail: `Resize` now returns `null` instead of asserting when called with a zero or negative dimension.
The Linux native loader uses the full library name of libc so it resolves on more distributions.
libjpeg-turbo bumped to 2.1.3, libwebp to 1.2.3, and libexpat to 2.4.8 — all pull in upstream security and correctness fixes.
The C API version was incremented to mark the surface changes in this release for downstream consumers of the native library.

contributor_summaries.jeromelaban: Landed the initial Skottie support, the WinUI target, and the missing Uno WinUI JavaScript support file
contributor_summaries.mgood7123: Exposed the AVIF image encode format
contributor_summaries.HarlanHugh: Fixed `SKGLView` reshape when the macOS window's backing scale factor changes
contributor_summaries.lindexi: Fixed font loading for fonts whose file names contain Unicode text
contributor_summaries.RichardD2: Guarded the internal critical section against being entered after finalization

preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.108: Preview 108 finished the .NET 6 TFM matrix and picked up the last fixes before stable.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.104: Preview 104 rolled up the native-library refreshes — libjpeg-turbo, libwebp, libexpat — and the finalized-lock guard.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.91: Preview 91 added Unicode font-file support, non-seekable Skottie streams and BOM-tolerant animations, and incremented the C API.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.79: Preview 79 rounded out the Skottie API with nullable attributes and `TimeSpan` overloads and the writable `SKDataStream`.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.75: Preview 75 landed the `SKGLView` macOS reshape fix.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.71: Preview 71 added the `net6.0-tizen` TFM and the Skottie/SceneGraph package split.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.63: Preview 63 was the big cut — WinUI support, Skottie, `SKShaper` text alignment, and the initial nullable/TFM refresh.
preview_summaries.v2.88.1-preview.1: Preview 1 opened the 2.88.1 line with documentation and CI fixes and the zlib update that resolved PNG loading.

harfbuzz_summary: HarfBuzzSharp 2.8.2.1 picks up the merged "classic" project layout, refreshed generator and the missing macOS/iOS feature implementation alongside the SkiaSharp packaging changes.
